_README: Per-entity ELT connector config — SCAFFOLD / TEMPLATE. Copy per entity, fill in, keep OUT of version control if you add real ids. CREDENTIALS ARE NEVER STORED HERE: only the NAMES of environment variables (and a token-store path) that hold them at runtime. Extraction facts + failure modes: ../knowledge/finance-elt-connector-facts.md. Staging transform: ../skills/finance-elt-staging/SKILL.md.

source_ids._note: Fill in ONLY the id for the chosen source_system; leave the rest null.

auth._rotating_refresh_token_note: TRUE for QBO and Xero — their refresh token ROTATES on every refresh. The new token MUST be persisted atomically (temp file + rename) BEFORE the new access token is used, under a per-entity file lock; alert on invalid_grant and follow the re-auth runbook. See ../knowledge/finance-elt-connector-facts.md.

extract._note: Advisory rate/pagination hints — VERIFY against the connector-facts settling gates before relying on them.
extract.rate_limit_hint: per source — QBO ~500 req/min/realmId; Xero 60/min + 5 concurrent + 5000/day/tenant; NetSuite concurrency ~1 default; Intacct XML readByQuery <=2000 rows/call
extract.page_size_hint: NetSuite SuiteQL ~1000 rows/page; Intacct readByQuery pagination via resultId

close_period_watermark.note: The close period this extract belongs to. Stamped on every staged row (the canonical `period` column) so the staging table is partitionable / incrementally loadable by close period. Override at stage time with --period.

output._canonical_columns_note: Byte-identical to the dbt stg_trial_balance model. Do not reorder or rename.
